title: coryphantha ramillosa subsp. santarosa
intro: Coryphantha ramillosa subsp. santarosa, also known as the Santa Rosa pincushion cactus, is a rare and endemic cactus species native to Baja California Peninsula in Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, colds, and other respiratory issues, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Coryphantha ramillosa subsp. santarosa include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and promoting wound healing. The plant can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and its medicinal properties can also be extracted and used in topical creams and ointments to promote skin health and speed up the healing process.
